titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 36/3/219

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=864 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:22:37.005404+00 | translation moa=865 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:22:35.790463+00

p.cl: . Rob’ de brus omnibus hominibus suis de valle de Anand’ Salutem .

Sciatis me reddidisse et concessisse Hugoni filio Ingenbaldi et
heredibus suis tenendum de me et heredibus meis totam terram quam pater suus tenuit in driuesdale . eadem seruitio et eisdem Libertatibus quibus pater suus Ingenbaldus eam ante ipsum tenuit . faciendo . s . mihi pro ea seruitium duarum villarum . et
in exercitu Regis  seruitium vnius Militis . Hanc terram predictam tenebit Hugo et heredes sui in nemore et plano .
in terra et aqua . In Monasteria et Molyndino . et in omnibus Libertatibus quibus pater suus eam melius et Liberius et quietius
tenuit ante ipsum .

Hiis Testibus . Domino Waldevo . Henrico murdac . Iuone de Sot’ . Willelmo de Gardino . Iuone de Crosobi .
Hadardo filio odonis . Roberto de Hodolm’ . Hugone Mauleuer’ . Hugone de Corri . Ric’ de Crossebi . Radulfo
de Grenesbi . Hamelino . T . clerico .
Robert de Brus to all his men of Annandale sends greeting.

Know me to have given again and granted to Hugh son of Ingenbald and his heirs to be held of me and my heirs the entire land which his father held in Dyfesdale, by the same service and the same liberties which his father Ingenbald held it before him, that is to say, by performing for it the service of two touns, and in the king's army the service of one knight. This aforesaid land shall Hugh hold, and his heirs, in wood and plain, in land and water, in monastery and mill, and in all liberties which his father well, freely, and quitly held it before him.

With these witnesses: the lord Waldeve, Henry Murdoch, Ivo de Sot', William de Jardin, Ivo of Crosbie, Udard son of Odo, Robert of Hoddom, Hugh Mauleverer, Hugh of Corrie, Richard of Crosbie, Ralph of Grainsby, Hamelin, T. the clerk.
